paper_title: Ostracod and Sediment Analysis in Alibunar Depression

methods.description: Process for screening sediment material for ostracod valves to infer palaeoenvironmental conditions.

steps.description: Sediment material was screened for ostracod valves at eight discrete intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: screen sediment material for ostracod valves

steps.description: About 5 ml sediment was extracted from 1 cm segments and gently wet-sieved on a 150 µm screen.
The residue was freeze-dried and ostracod valves were hand-picked under a stereo dissecting microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze-dry residue and hand-pick ostracod valves

steps.description: Identification keys and taxonomic papers were used to identify the ostracod species and infer palaeoenvironmental conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: identify ostracod species using taxonomic keys

methods.description: Procedure for luminescence dating of sediment samples and constructing an age-depth model.

steps.description: Eight samples of polymineral fine grains were taken from halved sediment core VRT1A and prepared for luminescence dating.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment samples for luminescence dating

steps.description: All measurements were carried out on an automated Risø TL/OSL DA 20 reader using the post-infrared infrared stimulated luminescence signal measured at 290°C.
steps.searchable_summary: perform luminescence measurements using Risø TL/OSL DA 20 reader

steps.description: Laboratory experiments included prior-IR stimulation temperature tests and preheat plateau and dose-recovery tests.
Equivalent doses were calculated with an arithmetic mean.
The resulting IR 50 and pIRIR 290 ages were used to construct an age-depth model using the approach of Zeeden et al. (2018).
steps.searchable_summary: construct age-depth model using luminescence ages

steps.description: AMS 14C dating of sediment bulk organic matter from two samples was carried out by BETA analytics.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS 14C dating on sediment samples
